details: The Padho Pardesh scheme is a central government program that provides interest subsidies to minority students who are pursuing master's, M.Phil, or Ph.D. degrees abroad. The objective of the scheme is to award interest subsidy to meritorious students belonging to economically weaker sections of notified minority communities so as to provide them better opportunities for higher education abroad and enhance their employability.
The subjects/ disciplines in which courses (for Masters, M.Phil and Ph.D only) may be undertaken for the grant of interest subsidy are listed below:-
Composite Materials Engineering including Decentralized power Distribution (for Solar Heat) system, Energy Storage Engineering, Energy conservation, Energy Efficient Habitat

Information Technology including Computer Engineering, Software, Software Quality assurance, Networking/ Connectivity Engineering, Communication system under Hazardous or Post-disaster conditions, Multi- media Communication
benefits: The scheme provides an interest subsidy on education loans, which can help to reduce the cost of overseas education for minority students.
The scheme makes overseas education more accessible to minority students from all backgrounds, regardless of their financial situation.
Studying abroad can give students a competitive edge in the job market, and the Padho Pardesh scheme can help minority students to achieve their career goals.
Studying abroad can be a transformative experience, and the Padho Pardesh scheme can help minority students to develop their skills and knowledge in a global context.
eligibility: Students must have been admitted to an approved master's, M.Phil, or Ph.D. program abroad.
Students must have been admitted to a recognized university or institution abroad.
Student's family income must not exceed ₹ 6,00,000 per annum.
Students must have obtained an education loan from a scheduled bank under the Indian Banks Association (IBA) Education Loan Scheme.
Students must apply for the scheme's benefits during their first year of study. Applications received in the second year or later will not be accepted.
Priority will be given to applications from states and union territories with lower bank interest rates.
Payment of financial benefits may be linked to the student's Aadhaar number.

faqs.question: If student has availed subsidy under any other scheme including the CSIS scheme for undergraduate courses, whether he/she will be eligible under this scheme?
faqs.answer: Student will be eligible, (as the interest subsidy) under the Scheme for pursuing Post Graduate Diploma, Post Graduate degree course/Masters, M.Phil & Ph.D. Further, those students, who have completed M.Tech in India but is now pursuing MBA abroad and these students have got subsidy under CSIS/ state subsidy scheme for the M.Tech course, are also be eligible in this Scheme.

faqs.question: Is Interest Subsidy guaranteed for all eligible applicants?
faqs.answer: No, interest subsidy is not guaranteed for all eligible applicants. However, it would be Ministry’s endeavor to provide interest subsidy to all eligible applicants to the extent possible. As per provision of scheme, total number of students to be selected in a year is 400.

faqs.question: As per Model Scheme of IBA, the maximum loan for study abroad is Rs.20 lakhs. Whether interest subsidy is admissible for loan amount disbursed above Rs.20.00 lakh?
faqs.answer: Presently, subsidy is restricted to the limits specified under IBA model scheme i.e. Rs 20.00 lakh only

faqs.question: Whether latest income certificate only should be obtained or the income certificate obtained during earlier years/ at the time of availing loan can be taken?
faqs.answer: Income Certificate issued by Competent Authority obtained at the time of availing loan, will be sufficient

faqs.question: What documents are required ?
faqs.answer: 1. Application form. 2. Admission letter. 3. Passport. 4. Income certificate. 5. Education loan application. 6. Aadhaar card.

faqs.question: What is the ‘Meritorious’ element in the Scheme for eligibility for interest subsidy?
faqs.answer: Those who have secured admission abroad and are eligible as per Scheme Guidelines will be considered as meritorious under the Scheme.

faqs.question: If the course pursued by the minority student is partly in India and partly abroad, shall be eligible for the interest subsidy?
faqs.answer: Such students will be eligible for interest subsidy, if the Degree is awarded by Foreign University.

faqs.question: What is the income limit under the scheme?
faqs.answer: Total income from all sources of the employed candidate or his/ her parents/guardians in case of unemployed candidate shall not exceed Rs. 6.00 lakh per annum. Income certificate issued by the competent authority in the State/ Union Territory should be submitted in support of income shown by a candidate.

faqs.question: When can a student apply for the scheme benefits?
faqs.answer: Students must apply for the scheme's benefits during their first year of study. Applications received in the second year or later will not be accepted.
